reviews.review_text: No updates for super long, despite the developers promises. Very disappointed. This game is abandoned.
Fun in creative but in survival it takes a bit to get started but after that is a breeze
Good enough game but it's been in early access since I was a kid so I can't really say it's worth it

Whilst it is a fun game, in 2026 it is entirely just 'Meet Potential Game!'. The day chapter 2 releases, not just yet another devlog saying 'Next year guys! Next year!" for the 5th year in a row, I will turn this review into a positive one. But with the current state of the game, and no proof of developers even existing beyond 1 devlog per year, it is a buggy, laggy mess which will never be finished. If you want to play this, download Create: Aeronautics instead, it's less buggy, less laggy, and feels about 10x more feature complete, and clearly has some degree of development behind it.

When it launched into Early Access, this game was awesome. It was novel, it was fun, just building things was an incredibly enjoyable experience. When survival released, it was great and a ton of fun. But ever since then, there's just been nothing. Nothing at all. There's a risk you run in purchasing an Early Access game, that the promise that the game will eventually release will be broken. Don't take the risk with this game, that promise has been broken.

Twas very fun.
I may not understand half of what is shown nor how to obtain the other half, but that's the fun of it! The progression (for me at least) is like Minecraft: up to the player's speed.

Used to be one of my favourite games, but has been abandoned. Wouldn't recommend buying unless you like the game in it's current state. Once you've beat the "endgame" stuff, there's not really much else to do but maybe build a bit more, get more resources, but what's the point really when the devs gave us this idea of more and they just dissapeared. It's a shame because I still enjoy this game, I just don't suppor the way they lied to their fanbase.
Game is unfortunately abandoned. They release nothing updates to avoid being labeled as abandoned by steam. We've been waiting on the new update for years.
I absolutely love this game and want to play it more we are just forever wating for any kind of update, let alone survival chapter 2
This game is great but it has a horrible update time and its filled with bugs. When I played in survival my I died and my bag got stuck in a wall and I spent almost an hour getting it out

Ignore my lack of hours, I've been watching development of the game for 7 years now from several Youtubers perspectives.

I want to believe the game is still being developed, but I'm afraid it might not be. The newest "Update" was a security update, which told me that they have no new content and just wanted to move the "Last Updated" date to more recently. It's sad to see being honest, with everyone begging and hoping the chapter 2 update will come somewhat soon. I've played survival and let me tell you, it is so bone dry. It's kind of the same trope of some survival horror games like "Misery" where it's so slow paced you get bored before anything interesting happens. Not even watching YouTube in the background could keep me from getting bored. I truly wish I could stay for the development, but I don't think my waiting will come to fruition. I'll watch from Kan or ScrapMan's perspective if and when chapter 2 comes out, Then I'll decide if I want to pick up the game then. But for now, I can't let $20 sit and rot for no reason. Sorry Axolot, I do think you are trying to get the update polished and ready, but be honest with the community instead of hiding behind your screen and hoping we don't notice. For crying out loud you forgot the 10th Anniversary of the game and the 6th Anniversary of survival, which is very sad. If you aren't working on the game anymore, just admit it. I'll be asking for a return of my money from steam and this review will stay up even after the update (If it happens) just to show the history Axolot's commitment.

It was a fun game I really liked all the actual storyline stuff they added towards the end but given the last update for this game was 2024 and they're only doing micro nothingburger updates every few months just to keep the "game hasn't been updated" warning from showing up I think it's safe to say this is probably going to be abandoned soon.

I have played this game to absolute death. Loved every second in creative mode. Hated every second in survival mode. None of the best stuff in the game will ever get any new content. Do not buy this game. You will only be disappointed.
Will always downvote prolonged early access games. "Believe it or not the next chapter is coming" give me a break.

My thoughts on ScrapMechanic varie, and I don't feel that my positive review of the game is completely accurate.
It has provided me with many hours of amazing gameplay. However, as the years have gone by, I have become disappointed with the lack of transparency from the development team.
If you think you'll enjoy the game as it stands right now, I do recommend it. It is a fabulous game. But that doesn't negate the fact that it has been slowly dying for the last half decade.

It's sad, but as things stand, I have lost my hope for the future of ScrapMechanic.

The game is fun to play with friends.
Survival is fun to explore in the first 2 hours but gets tedious later.
The devs dont want to revive the game with any sort of content update.

Its fine. Its dead. Its novel if you havent played anything like it!

this is an absolutely amazing game, i dont get why people dont recommend it because chapter 2 hasnt released yet.
its very fun to play, but does tank the cpu a bit when making something more complex

It's a good game, dare I say a great game. However the issue isn't with the game, it's with the Dev team. Six years ago as this rating is being typed, Chapter 2 would be announced... six years ago. They said Chapter 2's release date would be in the coming months as of new years for 2026, or 2025 (I'm not sure which on it is). It's been about five months, almost six, and we still don't have a release date. The community will die, and thus the game will die without updates, both game and news wise. I would still recommend the game, just give it a bad review, maybe we'll get their attention that way.

Scrap Mechanic might be one of the greatest creations of all time. The game is able to be played at any technicality level. If you want it to be an exploration game, its an exploration game. If you want it to be a first person shooter, its a first person shooter. If you want it to be a game about meticulous robotical creations and optimization, it can be that. 
       At the time of review, the game is still a WIP and chapter 2 has not yet been release. The style of the game feels intentionally goofy, however the game as a whole is not poorly made. I would, and do recommend this game to anyone who is interested in survival games, sandbox games, and multiplayer games. 10/10
I love this game. It’s very fun, but I might be a little crazy because I still hold out hope that they’ll update it someday.
I can't tell if this game is abandoned or the devs are horrible at Communicating But this review is staying negative until chapter 2 comes out.
Don't buy, it's in development hell until further notice and feels unfinished.
It's great if you love building stupid things or impressive things, or if you wanna challenge your brain or do some fun survival stuff. Runs amazingly too

I've played a decent bit of Scrap Mechanic and it's a really fun game! The one drawback (and it is a BIG drawback) is that Chapter 2 isn't released yet. Personally, I've only been playing during two "Hey! Chapter 2'll be here this year!.... Sorry, it won't be out this year actually :(" announcements, but Scrap Mechanic has too much potential to give up on.
ITS FUN UNTIL YOU COMPLETETLY RUN OUT OF CONTENT, about 15 hours into gameplay.

The game is very good, they completely nailed the basics and created a solid foundation to build something outstanding, but now it appears like it is abandoned, with no meaningful updates for over a year, and very little communication from the devs. I really hope the game is not dead and that everything in the dev updates will finally be pushed to the public, but for now the survival mode is clearly unfinished, and just leaves me wishing there was more to it. i will obviously change my review if/when the update drops
Too empty, survival adds food and water but thats it, not worth $20
Very comprehensive building, open world, no specific progression path. Very fun, and the building is super entertaining.
we have been waiting and waiting for chapter 2 and theres still absolutely no sign of it ever releasing because the devs are dead silent about updates
peak game, as someone with 1k+ hours buy it its worth your time. Axolot where is chapter 2?
this game is a love and hate topic for many players. gta 6 before scrap mechanic update will hit hard
Highly recommend this game for engineering. Needs some work for water physics and more vanilla additions (such as a steam engine or functioning propellers), but otherwise - an awesome game!

Good game made feel like a dirty grease monkey stranded with his 2 dumb friends that don't understand anything about making a working car. How ever they were able to put 4 wheels on woody the wooden pecker. 10/10 bring some one with a brain though.

It's an amazing game. had it since pre survival days. wish chapter 2 would come out already, been huffing hoipum so much that i think i build up a tolerance to it. can't wait for chapter 2, hope it arrives before im on my deathbed

👍 Recommended
🕒 4.2 hours on record  

Scrap Mechanic is a fantastic sandbox game that gives you incredible freedom to build almost anything you can imagine. With 4.2 hours on record, I’ve only scratched the surface, but it’s already clear why so many people love it.

The building mechanics are deep, satisfying, and very well made. You can create everything from simple vehicles to insane contraptions, working machines, or silly inventions. The game shines especially in Creative mode, where you can let your imagination run wild without any limitations. Survival mode takes a bit longer to get going, but once you get established it becomes very enjoyable.

The graphics are colorful and charming, performance is solid, and the physics are excellent. The game gives you total freedom — you can play it as a building game, a survival game, a racing game, or even a shooter if you want.

The only real downside right now is that the game is still waiting for Chapter 2, so some players feel like development is moving a bit slowly.

Overall, if you enjoy building, inventing, and messing around with physics, Scrap Mechanic is a must-have. One of the most creative and entertaining sandbox games available.  

Highly recommended! ⚙️🛠️👍
I've run out of stuff to do. Now I just try to improve on the creations I've built and also deforest the entire map. I think I've gotten my moneys worth but still wanting chapter 2.

Really love this game, its ntuitive and just generally very very fun. creations can get quite laggy really quick, dont know if that will ever not be a problem, but all in all, my favourite sandbox game.

great game and id love to continue playing but we've been waiting actual years for chapter 2 would i recommend buying it, yeah its really fun and creativity is endless but when will they actually stop teasing us once in a life time and come back to communicating with the community and actively work on chapter 2, this is one of my fav games ever and hell i like this game a whole lot more than raft it shouldve been Scrap Mechanic being focused on with development not raft u got lightning in a bottle with this game dont let it go like u have been come back and update the game more.

Its really fun and fresh compared to other games, and you get to learn as you play, but not everything is just handed to you. in order to get a bigger car you have to drive around and fight for your food at night just to survive. Build your way up and fight stronger Bots with Spudguns and machines.
I love the simplicity and ability to do everything i want in game

I can't explain how good this game is, I've played this for 1000 hours. You could build anything with just training. I play creative mode because I like to build stuff. The survival map is really big and you could download Maps, vehicle, mods. You could build your own maps. This is better than trailmakers because it has no build limitations. If you a farmer, you can build a gigantic farm of every plant. If you like space you could build space-ships. You could build a flying waffle. only you're imagination limites you. I'M SERIOUS.

If you don't like survival and you want a challenge you could play a gamemode that makes you build vehicles that are built for a specific occasions.

If you want to torture bots (Monsters) you could build machines anything to kill them. In survival you need to build walls to protect your home from bots and you will need to build a gun, auto aming gun. you could also build a B-2 (Bomber plane) to drop bombs on them. The game is co-op. so you and your friends could have war.

PLEASE PLAY. It's so fun when you play it

I haven't laughed as hard as I have playing Scrap Mechanic with a friend of mine in what's gotta be years by this point - I recommend the game for that alone. Other than that, like many others, the game was a core part of my childhood as I spent tons of time watching various YouTubers play the game and experiment with the core mechanics - all of which have, in the time since 2016, been expertly refined. 

It's honestly pretty surreal playing this game in 2026, lord knows 2016 me would be elated if they knew how far this game would go. Sure - it might seem like it's been pretty much abandoned, but hell I'm havin' fun so who cares. 

TLDR: Fun & intuitive game, plenty of good childhood memories watching YouTube videos of it; and I'm enjoying the process of making new memories now that I've got a copy for myself. 10/10
I love this game, this allows me express my creativity and makes me think outside the box, if you love engineering games this is for you
PLEASE AXOLOTL CHAPTER 2. also this game lwk slaps but is grindy, kinda scratches the nut between my cheeks
This is an amazing game, the amount of parts it gives you makes it feel like there is no limit to what you can make.

Amazing game very good. Slow aah developers but I like the game. Multiplayer needs some working or it might be my potato pc and my friends dog water internet. I recommend trying it if you like building random creations and fooling around with cool physics. Speaking of physics, it could be better but it gets the job done for most creations. BIG CREATIONS LAG. Different creations colliding also used to lag like crazy but they fixed it. Im hoping they will fix big creation lags too soon

I have played this game for almost 1000 hours this game was my first ever pc game this game helped me through my pc gaming journey but besides all of that stuff the game is actually really great the survival mode is great the creative mode is great the challenge mode is ok but I would recommend it to anyone who is into creation of machines the warehouses in survival mode are kind of difficult you have to grind a lot in this game this game is high risk high reward in survival mode so you have to risk a lot to get a lot you also have to grind a lot and to get far you have to spend hours on it
Great game. Spend the last three or four years dedicated into it. But the devs seems to gave up on the updates.

European children born on release date are going into high school next year by the way.

As eager as I am to recommend it for all the fun times I've had in this game over my years of playing, I can't. It's abandonware in all but confirmation.

Don't fall for it, Resist temptation, Refuse to buy this game until it's done. It's the original example of why not to buy anything in early access. Strangle their funding funnel and brute force a release!

I had high hopes for this game and it was loads of fun but it is still hammering a 10900K CPU harder that AAA titles. My now older but still powerful CPU is not worth cooking long term to play this game. I really wish the DEVs would optimize the code. They clearly understand logic etc. I've used this to teach my 10 year old about logic gates. I think in general this is an awesome game but I simply cannot ignore the fact that the DEVs are reluctant to fix the optimization for years and have uninstalled it.
its obvioualy a work in progress. fun game, i do wish there was switches to hinges though

998.7 hours on this game is crazy compared to other reviews and the total play time for other players and they have like 500-700 hours and just think will the second chapter come out before gta6 and if Axolot games does what the CEO or whoever it is of take 2 games and Axolot charged 3.25 and lest say the by the time you would see this i would have 1000 hours on this game it would be $3,250 be glad the developer / publisher does not charge by the hour this game is just great
It has been almost 6 months maybe more and we have not got a update for the release date, the year is near half way over and no update, can we get a update on the progress already
building is great, survival mode is great, love it

ive heard a lot about this game when it came into early access and its still just as amazing as it was back then. i know a lot of people (me included) have been waiting years and lots of people are getting upset at how long its taking, tho i will still send my support till i dont see any updates. the next chapter they are adding sounds massive, like doubling the game size kinda massive, and i think that the devs maybe just over estimated themselves.
